seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Stone Well and Stonyfell. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Stone Well vs Stonyfell: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Stone Well and Stonyfell.
heading: Stone Well vs Stonyfell — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Stone Well, South Australia and Stonyfell, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Stone Well has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Stonyfell (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Stone Well or Stonyfell safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Stone Well has a flood risk score of 33/100, while Stonyfell scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Stone Well scores 62/100 for bushfire risk. Stonyfell scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Stone Well or Stonyfell?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Stone Well has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.48% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
